changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_tls_enforcement to k8s_networkpolicy_tls_required for clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to secure information flow. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but a minor change was made to improve clarity and consistency. The functions are technically accurate and applicable to Kubernetes.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'required' instead of 'enforcement' for clarity in k8s_networkpolicy_tls_enforcement
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | process requirements for information transfer whe
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(32) focuses on enforcing secure information flow, particularly through encryption and secure transport mechanisms. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that ingress traffic is secured with TLS, service mesh configurations enforce mTLS for secure service-to-service communication, network policies enforce secure transport, and API server communications are encrypted with TLS. These checks align with the requirement to enforce secure information transfer processes.
